subject: Databases
title: Foreign Key Cascades
description: A fast breakdown of Foreign Key Cascades for builders.

hook.text: Delete a customer who has orders. ON DELETE CASCADE removes the orders too. Automatic cleanup.

buildup.text: CASCADE options: ON DELETE CASCADE, ON DELETE SET NULL, ON DELETE RESTRICT. Each handles orphans differently.

discovery.text: RESTRICT blocks the delete entirely. SET NULL clears the foreign key. CASCADE deletes the child rows.

twist.text: Cascading deletes can be dangerous. Delete one parent row and hundreds of children vanish silently.

climax.text: Use RESTRICT for safety in most cases. Use CASCADE only when child data is meaningless without the parent.

punchline.text: CASCADE is powerful. RESTRICT is safer. Choose deliberately.

quiz.question: When is ON DELETE CASCADE appropriate?
options: When child records have no meaning without the parent row
Whenever you want to save disk space
It should always be used as the default
